data.text: I’m reading the bead contract and the referenced evidence first, then I’ll trace each flagged symbol back to its callers before deciding wire vs delete. After that I’ll make the smallest scoped edits, run the required Go tests and deadcode check, and write the decisions log in the execution directory.

data.text: I’ve got the main resolver files open. Now I’m checking whether each flagged symbol is truly dead or just hidden behind gqlgen/server wiring, because the deadcode tool only cares about production reachability from `main()`, not local test helpers.

data.text: I’ve confirmed `NewResolver` is referenced by server construction in `cli/internal/server/server.go`, but deadcode still flags it, which means the production entry path from `main()` probably never reaches that constructor. I’m checking the `main -> server -> GraphQL` chain next so I can decide whether to wire the constructor in or remove dead helpers instead of guessing.

data.text: The GraphQL handler is built in `cli/internal/server/server.go`, so the remaining question is whether the CLI’s production entry path actually calls that server package. I’m checking the command entry points now; if not, the right fix may be to delete genuinely dead GraphQL helpers and leave the runtime wiring alone.
